title: acer opalus subsp. hispanicum
intro: Acer opalus subsp. hispanicum is a subspecies of the field maple tree (Acer opalus), a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The bark and leaves of Acer opalus subsp. hispanicum contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditional herbal preparations of Acer opalus subsp. hispanicum include teas and infusions made from the bark and leaves, which can be used to alleviate headaches, joint pain, and other health complaints.
